reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Tuesday 23 June
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Wednesday 17 June to Monday 22 June.
reports.summary: The week from Wednesday 17 June to Monday 22 June averaged about 24.0 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 40.5% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Friday 19 June, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 48.8% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Monday 22 June and lowest on Friday 19 June.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Friday 19 June, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 48.8% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 7.6 GW average output on Wednesday 17 June.
Solar was strongest on Sunday 21 June, averaging about 4.5 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 8.7 GW across the period, rising highest on Monday 22 June and falling lowest on Friday 19 June.
Measured generation was highest on Monday 22 June, at roughly 26.8 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Friday 19 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Friday 19 June had the strongest renewable share at 48.8%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Friday 19 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 7.6 GW on Friday 19 June. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Monday 22 June
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 10.6 GW on Monday 22 June. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 7.6 GW on Wednesday 17 June.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 4.5 GW on Sunday 21 June.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 10.6 GW on Monday 22 June; lowest was about 7.6 GW on Friday 19 June.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Monday 22 June
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Monday 22 June, measured average generation was about 26.8 GW. Renewables averaged 38.0% of measured generation, gas averaged 10.6 GW, wind averaged 5.5 GW and solar averaged 4.2 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 48.8% average renewable share on Friday 19 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 7.6 GW average wind output on Wednesday 17 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 4.5 GW average solar output on Sunday 21 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 10.6 GW average gas output on Monday 22 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 48.8% on Friday 19 June
• Highest average wind output: ~7.6 GW on Wednesday 17 June
• Highest average solar output: ~4.5 GW on Sunday 21 June
• Highest average gas output: ~10.6 GW on Monday 22 June

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-06-23/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
